Ali Aziz

Mechanical Engineer | M.Sc. Candidate | Heat Transfer & Nanofluids Researcher

Mechanical Engineering graduate and current Master's student at Kuwait University, interested in heat transfer, HVAC systems, nanofluids, enhanced oil recovery experiments, and the application of AI/ML in engineering research.

About Me

I am a Mechanical Engineer with a strong academic and research interest in thermal systems, heat exchangers, nanofluids, HVAC applications, and experimental engineering. My current research focuses on the thermal performance and fouling behavior of hybrid nanofluids in double-pipe heat exchangers. I am also developing my skills in Python, data analysis, machine learning, and AI-assisted research to support experimental prediction and modeling.

Education

M.Sc. in Mechanical Engineering

Kuwait University | Current

Research focus: Heat transfer enhancement, hybrid nanofluids, fouling behavior, and experimental analysis of double-pipe heat exchangers.

B.Sc. in Mechanical Engineering

Kuwait University

Graduated with Excellent with Honors. Capstone project focused on an innovative tire recycling system integrating multiple mechanical stages to recover rubber and steel.

Experience

Research Assistant

Enhanced Oil Recovery Center, Kuwait University

Working on petroleum engineering laboratory experiments and research activities including core flooding, interfacial tension measurements, gas chromatography, PVT-related systems, contact angle measurements, and experimental setup preparation.

Mechanical Engineering Trainee / Early Career Experience

Industrial and academic environments

Gained exposure to mechanical systems, research documentation, equipment preparation, laboratory work, and technical reporting.

Selected Projects

Hybrid Nanofluids in Double-Pipe Heat Exchangers

Master's thesis project investigating the heat transfer performance and fouling behavior of hybrid nanofluids in a controlled experimental double-pipe heat exchanger setup.

Innovative Tire Recycling System

Capstone project involving the design of a mechanical system that integrates multiple processing stages to recycle tires and recover useful rubber and steel materials.

Cooling Load Analysis

Conducted air-conditioning load calculations using both manual methods and HAP software as part of advanced HVAC coursework.
